timeline.detail: Scenario state frozen at t=36s. COA A applied: ftr-n1/n2 scramble toward trk-h01, ftr-h1/h2 scramble toward trk-h02.
ftr-n1 and ftr-n2 airborne from Base North. Heading to intercept trk-h01.
ftr-h1 and ftr-h2 airborne from Highridge. Heading to intercept trk-h02.
ftr-n1 achieves weapons lock on trk-h01 at (220, 495). Range 35 km. Firing.
trk-h01 intercepted. Kill confirmed. ftr-n1 expends 2 air-to-air missiles.
ftr-h1 achieves weapons lock on trk-h02 at (315, 450). Range 30 km. Firing.
trk-h02 intercepted. Kill confirmed. ftr-h1 expends 2 air-to-air missiles.
trk-h03 (UAV) continues slow approach. No engagement ordered. Monitoring.
ftr-n1 and ftr-n2 returning to Base North. Estimated landing t=95s. Recovery and rearm: 25 minutes.
ftr-h1 and ftr-h2 returning to Highridge. Estimated landing t=100s. Recovery and rearm: 25 minutes.

All high-priority threats neutralized. No zone breaches. 4 fighters in recovery. 9 assets available (2 North, 1 Highridge, 2 UAV, 2 South standby, 2 Spear QRA). Both SAM batteries unused and fully loaded.

narration: Simulation complete. Option A successfully intercepted both fighter tracks before they reached the Arktholm defence zone. ftr-n1 engaged trk-h01 at 220 km east, 495 km north — well outside the zone boundary. ftr-h1 engaged trk-h02 shortly after. No zone breaches, no asset losses. Four fighters are now in recovery and will need 25 minutes before they're available again. That leaves us with 9 assets including the QRA pair at Spear Point and both SAM batteries fully loaded. The key vulnerability: if a second wave arrives in the next 25 minutes, we're operating at 69% readiness with our two most experienced pairs unavailable.
